page: Microsoft Azure F4as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Fasv7 server family. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external network-attached volumes. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it does not feature any hardware GPU accelerators.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F4as_v7 offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources within a dedicated CPU framework. This configuration is designed for workloads that require consistent CPU performance without the overhead of local storage. Suitable workloads include general-purpose applications, web serving, small-scale databases, and development environments.
description: Microsoft Azure F4as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Fasv7 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external storage volumes. The instance also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile makes the F4as_v7 a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources with a standard memory footprint. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and development environments that rely on external persistent storage.
